summary: Ukrainian reactive UAVs and Shahed missiles are reported across multiple front‑line regions, while Russian forces announce a MiG‑31K launch carrying a Kinzhal missile toward Kyiv. Significant infrastructure damage includes a fire at the Kerch sea port and continued shortages of electricity and fuel in occupied Crimea. Both sides claim aerial losses, with Russia reporting 239 Ukrainian drones downed and Ukraine reporting numerous Shahed strikes.
key_points: Ukrainian reactive UAVs and Shahed missiles were observed over Kyiv, Chernihiv, Zhytomyr and other regions, some heading toward Kyiv from Chernihiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67966).
A Russian MiG‑31K fighter, carrier of an X‑47M2 Kinzhal missile, was launched from Savasleyka airfield, prompting a nationwide air alarm (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67964).
A fire broke out at the Kerch sea port after a massed Ukrainian attack, with additional fires reported at the Caucasus port and a military unit (Source: @UkraineNow/82086).
Occupied Crimea reports a total loss of electricity and gasoline supplies (Source: @eRadarrua/97635).
Russian authorities claim 239 Ukrainian drones were shot down over Russian regions overnight (Source: @tass_agency/381680).
Four civilians were killed and 28 injured by a Ukrainian drone attack on the Kerch peninsula, halting ferry services (Source: @tass_agency/381680).
Ukrainian Shahed attacks on Zaporizhzhia resulted in one injured at a local gas station (Source: @UkraineNow/82093).
Russian forces evacuated residents of Konstantinovka in Belgorod region (Source: @tass_agency/381704).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian reactive UAVs and Shahed missiles are reported over Kyiv and several northern oblasts, with a Russian MiG‑31K launch carrying a Kinzhal missile toward the capital. Major infrastructure damage includes a fire at the Kerch sea port and continued utility outages in occupied Crimea. Both sides claim significant aerial losses, Russia reporting 239 Ukrainian drones downed and Ukraine noting multiple Shahed strikes.

points: Reactive UAVs were sighted over Kyiv, prompting an air alarm at 10:32 and again at 12:18 on 21 June 2026 (Source: @eRadarrua/97608, @eRadarrua/97643).
Shahed missiles were reported near Vysokyi/Obolon district heading toward Lukianivka, and 8 Shaheds over Izmail (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67980, @mon1tor_ua/67962).
Ukrainian forces claimed a strike on a postal terminal in a Kharkiv suburb, damaging containers, trucks and the building (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31053).
Ukrainian artillery groups reported multiple strike groups in Kyiv, Sumy, Chernihiv and other oblasts, with specific counts of strike groups (Source: @eRadarrua/97610).
A Ukrainian drone attack on Zaporizhzhia gas station injured one person (Source: @UkraineNow/82093).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed moving from Chernihiv toward Kyiv, from Bryansk toward Chernihiv, and from Kharkiv northward (Source: @kpszsu/65866, @kpszsu/65865, @kpszsu/65876).
Ukrainian Shahed activity was reported over multiple villages in Zhytomyr and Rivne regions, including 1 Shahed over Vysokyi and 1 over Boryspil (Source: @eRadarrua/97607).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed strike near the Kerch peninsula causing civilian casualties (Source: @tass_agency/381680).
A Russian MiG‑31K fighter, carrier of an X‑47M2 Kinzhal missile, took off from Savasleyka airfield (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67964).
Russian authorities announced a nationwide air alarm due to the MiG‑31K launch (Source: @eRadarrua/97644).
Russia reported shooting down 239 Ukrainian drones over its territory overnight (Source: @tass_agency/381680).
Four civilians were killed and 28 injured by a Ukrainian drone attack on the Kerch peninsula, halting ferry operations (Source: @tass_agency/381680).
Four people died and 28 were injured in a Ukrainian drone attack on the Kerch peninsula, with ferry services suspended (Source: @tass_agency/381680).
Russian forces evacuated residents of Konstantinovka in Belgorod region (Source: @tass_agency/381704).
A Russian drone was downed over Sevastopol; no casualties were reported (Source: @tass_agency/381686).
Russian officials reported 14 injured, including two children, from a Ukrainian drone strike in Crimea (Source: @tass_agency/381684).
Ukrainian Shahed missiles were observed moving south‑west past Sosnytsia and Makoshyne in Chernihiv region (Source: @kpszsu/65855).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported heading from Bryansk toward Gorbuny and Ripky in Chernihiv (Source: @kpszsu/65866).
Multiple UAV groups were seen over Kharkiv from the north and from Bryansk heading toward Bohodukhiv (Source: @kpszsu/65865, @kpszsu/65876).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over Sumy heading toward Konotop and Dubovyazivka (Source: @kpszsu/65877).
A group of reactive UAVs was reported in central Chernihiv heading toward Olishivka and Honcharivske (Source: @kpszsu/65860).
Ukrainian Shahed activity was reported near the village of Gorbuny in Chernihiv (Source: @eRadarrua/97642).
Ukrainian UAVs were sighted over the northern part of Kharkiv region, including near Shevchenko and Chuhuyiv (Source: @kpszsu/65879).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported moving from the south of Zhytomyr toward Shpytky and other villages (Source: @kpszsu/65856).
Ukrainian reactive UAVs were reported over Kyiv and its suburbs, including Vysokyi, Boryspil and Ivankiv (Source: @eRadarrua/97607).
Shahed missiles were observed near Izmail and Kilija in Odesa region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67961).
Ukrainian UAVs were sighted over Rivne from the north and heading toward Sarny (Source: @eRadarrua/97642).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported over Zhytomyr heading toward Chudniv, Pulyne and Chernyakhiv (Source: @eRadarrua/97642).
Ukrainian Shahed activity was reported near the village of Vylkove in Odesa (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67961).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over the north of Kherson heading toward Mykolaiv (Source: @kpszsu/65881).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported over the north of Sumy heading toward Khutir-Mykhailivskyi and Svesa (Source: @kpszsu/65885).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over the north of Chernihiv heading toward Olishivka (Source: @kpszsu/65860).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported from Kherson heading toward Mykolaiv, course to Bereznehuvate (Source: @kpszsu/65881).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed from the south of Kherson heading toward Velyka Dymirka and Dymyr (Source: @eRadarrua/97607).
Ukrainian Shahed missiles were reported near the Kerch sea port, causing a large fire (Source: @UkraineNow/82086).
Ukrainian UAV activity was reported over Zaporizhzhia from the south (Source: @kpszsu/65858).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported over the southern part of Sumy heading toward Kharkiv (Source: @kpszsu/65860).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed moving from the south of Zhytomyr toward Khmelnytskyi and Shepetivka (Source: @kpszsu/65869).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported over the southern part of Kharkiv heading toward Velyka Dymirka (Source: @eRadarrua/97607).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over the southern part of Mykolaiv region (Source: @eRadarrua/97619).
Russia announced a nationwide air alarm after a MiG‑31K launch carrying a Kinzhal missile (Source: @eRadarrua/97644).
Russian officials reported shooting down 239 Ukrainian drones over Russian territory overnight (Source: @tass_agency/381680).
Ukrainian Shahed missiles were reported over multiple oblasts, including Kyiv, Zhytomyr, and Odesa (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67966, @mon1tor_ua/67962).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed heading from Bryansk (Russia) toward Chernihiv and from Kharkiv northward (Source: @kpszsu/65866, @kpszsu/65876).
A Ukrainian drone strike on the Kerch peninsula caused civilian casualties and halted ferry services (Source: @tass_agency/381680).
Russian forces reported a downed Ukrainian drone in Sevastopol with no casualties (Source: @tass_agency/381686).
Ukrainian UAV activity was reported over the southern front, including Kherson and Mykolaiv (Source: @kpszsu/65881).
Russian air defenses reportedly intercepted multiple Shahed missiles over Kyiv and surrounding areas (Source: @eRadarrua/97653).

key_developments: Ukrainian reactive UAVs and Shahed missiles were observed over Kyiv and surrounding oblasts, some originating from Chernihiv, indicating increased pressure on the capital (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67966).
A Russian MiG‑31K fighter launched a Kinzhal missile from Savasleyka, prompting a nationwide air alarm across Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67964).
A large fire erupted at the Kerch sea port after a massed Ukrainian attack, with additional fires at nearby facilities (Source: @UkraineNow/82086).
Occupied Crimea reports a total loss of electricity and gasoline supplies, worsening civilian hardship (Source: @eRadarrua/97635).
Russia claims to have shot down 239 Ukrainian drones over its territory in a single night (Source: @tass_agency/381680).
Four civilians killed and 28 injured by a Ukrainian drone strike on the Kerch peninsula, halting ferry operations (Source: @tass_agency/381680).
Ukrainian Shahed attack on a Zaporizhzhia gas station injured one person (Source: @UkraineNow/82093).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian UAV and Shahed activity is intensifying across the northern and central fronts, targeting Kyiv and critical infrastructure, while Russian air defenses remain strained. The deployment of a Kinzhal missile from a MiG‑31K indicates Russia's willingness to use strategic weapons against Ukrainian targets, raising the risk of high‑value strikes. Russian claims of extensive UAV losses suggest effective air defense but may also reflect heightened operational tempo on both sides.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities are issuing repeated air alerts and mobilizing anti‑aircraft assets in Kyiv and surrounding oblasts. Russian regional commands report evacuations in border settlements and increased air defense alerts following the Kinzhal launch. Both sides are leveraging propaganda to highlight aerial successes, influencing domestic and international perceptions.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued monitoring of UAV flight paths from Russian‑held territories into Ukrainian airspace is essential for early warning. The concentration of Shahed missiles near key logistics hubs suggests a focus on disrupting supply lines. The reported utility outages in Crimea could be exploited for psychological operations, while the Kinzhal launch underscores the need to track high‑speed missile trajectories.
